beats.narration_text: Selam — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: lyric-resync, the skill that takes an existing music video whose visuals are great but drift from the words, and rebuilds it so every clip lands on its lyric line. One beat, one lyric, one source still, one Hailuo clip.

props.runningText: 1 beat = 1 lyric line = 1 still = 1 Hailuo clip...
output: Phase 1: librosa beat grid → master clock
Phase 2: faster-whisper forced alignment → word timing
Phase 3: sharpest frame per beat → stills/
Phase 4: look at still + lyric → write prompt → Hailuo

beats.narration_text: The skill has two sibling scripts: analyze audio from the music-video skill for the beat grid, and align lyrics from the same for word-level timing. Its own scripts are extract frames, pick stills by sharpness, and generate video songbird for the Hailuo image-to-video pass. The beat sheet is the shared contract. The key new field is the Phase four prompt pair: image prompt and video prompt, each written by examining the chosen still and its lyric line together.

props.title: Reuse the visuals. Obey the words.
files: scripts/extract_frames.sh — frames at ~1fps from source video
scripts/pick_stills.py — sharpest frame per beat window
scripts/generate_video_songbird.sh — Hailuo clip per beat, cut to duration
props.calloutSub: written by looking at both still and lyric

beats.narration_text: Six phase-gated steps: lock audio timing with librosa; lock lyric timing with faster-whisper forced alignment — gate zero before touching frames; extract frames and pick the sharpest per beat window — gate one to eyeball the stills; write the image and video prompt by looking at each still and its lyric; generate the Hailuo clip and gate two to approve it; assemble all clips in beat order and mux the original audio. At every gate, regenerate only the failing unit.

props.title: Audio lock → still pick → prompt → clip → assemble.
props.inputLabel: existing music video + audio + lyrics (plain text)
props.outputLabel: beat-synced reel with captions (original audio preserved)

phases.label: AUDIO+LYRIC
phases.note: librosa + faster-whisper → beat grid + word timing — GATE 0 before frames

phases.label: STILLS
phases.note: extract frames ~1fps; pick_stills.py keeps sharpest per beat — GATE 1: eyeball

phases.label: PROMPTS
phases.note: Phase 4: look at still + lyric → image_prompt + video_prompt (vision step)

phases.label: CLIPS
phases.note: Hailuo image→video at 10s; center-trim to exact beat duration — GATE 2: approve

phases.label: ASSEMBLE
phases.note: concat clips in beat order + mux original WAV + Remotion caption overlay

props.footerNote: Video costs more than stills. Never generate a Hailuo clip until the still is approved at Gate 1. Regenerate only the failing unit — not the batch.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: Phase four prompt writing for one beat from Scarborough Fair, traditional English folk song, public domain. One still description, one lyric line, one prompt pair.

props.runningText: examining still + lyric — writing prompt pair...
output: source: Scarborough Fair (traditional English, public domain)
beat: B03  lyric: 'Parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme'
Phase 1-3: require WAV + video — not run here

beats.narration_text: Beat three. Still: a woman in a grey-green linen dress stands in a herb garden at golden hour, her back to camera, hand trailing along lavender stems. Lyric: parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme. The still and the line already belong together — the prompt preserves that. Image prompt: from this frame, the woman still facing away in the herb garden, golden light deepening on the sage and rosemary to the left of frame; the lavender's grey-green cool against warm soil. Video prompt: the same scene, subject stationary; the camera drifts left past the herb rows, settling on the rosemary and thyme in soft focus; land the depth before the cut. Duration 5.2 seconds. Never put the lyric text in the prompt. Describe the picture.

props.title: PHASE 4 — Scarborough Fair B03
props.code:
beat_id: B03
lyric_text: "Parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme"
start_s: 9.1  end_s: 14.3  duration_s: 5.2

still: woman in grey-green linen, herb garden,
  golden hour, back to camera, trailing lavender

image_prompt:
  "from this frame: subject facing away in herb
  garden; golden light deepens on sage + rosemary;
  lavender grey-green cool against warm soil"

video_prompt:
  "same scene, subject stationary; camera drifts
  left past herb rows, settling on rosemary + thyme
  in soft focus; land the depth before the cut;
  duration 5.2s"

rule: preserve subject/pose/composition
  push expression+light toward lyric meaning
  NEVER put lyric text in the prompt

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: the audio is the master clock. Beat timing comes from the real waveform via librosa — never from a word-count guess. If beat three lasts 5.2 seconds, the Hailuo clip is requested at ten seconds and center-trimmed to 5.2 seconds. A word-count estimate might say beat three is 4 seconds long. The lyric says three words. The waveform says the singer held the last word for three beats. The waveform wins. Always.

props.heading: Beat timing from the waveform. Never guessed.
props.body: librosa runs on the mastered WAV and produces the beat grid: BPM, beat timestamps, downbeat timestamps, durationInFrames. Every clip length, every caption window, every Hailuo request derives from this grid. Forced alignment (faster-whisper) puts each sung word on its real frame — not on a proportional distribution of the beat. If the singer held a note, the timing knows it. A word-count estimate doesn't.
props.quote: The audio is the master clock.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: Phase four is a vision step. The image prompt and video prompt for each beat are written by opening the chosen still and reading the lyric line — both, at the same time. Not the lyric alone — that produces a prompt that fights the source image. Not the still alone — that misses what the line means. The match is the decision that a human or model eye looked at both the picture and the words and decided they belong together.

props.heading: Look at both. Still and lyric. Then write.
props.body: Phase 4 is not a script — it is a vision step. For every beat: open the chosen still, read the lyric line, decide they belong together, then write the image_prompt (preserve subject/pose/composition; push expression+light toward what the lyric means) and the video_prompt (same scene + one camera move, key moment early, cuts cleanly to the beat). Never put the lyric text in the prompt. Describe the picture.
props.quote: a human-or-model eye looked at both the picture and the words and decided they belong together

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: gates and single-unit regeneration. The pipeline has two explicit gates: gate zero locks the audio and lyric timing before touching frames; gate one is a still eyeball before generating any Hailuo clips; gate two approves each clip before assembly. At every gate, regenerate only the failing unit — not the whole batch. A rejected still regenerates one still. A rejected clip regenerates one clip. The stills that passed gate one are not touched again. Video costs more than stills — the gate prevents spending Hailuo budget on already-approved work.

props.heading: Regenerate the failing unit. Not the batch.
props.body: Gate 0: audio + lyric timing locked before frames. Gate 1: stills eyeballed before clips. Gate 2: each clip approved before assembly. At every gate, the rule is: regenerate only the unit that failed. A rejected still in beat 7 does not invalidate the stills in beats 1–6. A rejected clip in beat 3 does not trigger re-generation of approved clips. Stills are cheap; Hailuo clips cost more. The gate exists to prevent spending on already-approved work.
props.quote: regenerate only the unit that failed, never the whole batch

beats.narration_text: Lyric-resync: audio-first timing, a vision step for each prompt, and single-unit regeneration at every gate. The waveform is the clock. The still and the lyric are both required. The gate is before the spend.

artifactLines: librosa beat grid: master clock for every clip length
Phase 4 vision step: look at still + lyric before writing prompt
gate before Hailuo: approve still first, regenerate only the failing unit

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run lyric-resync on an existing video. Before you start: is the audio locked — have you run librosa and confirmed the beat grid before extracting any frames? And for each beat, are you opening the still and reading the lyric line together before writing the prompt, or are you writing the prompt from the lyric alone?

props.runningText: audio locked? still + lyric together before the prompt?
output: audio locked? run librosa before extracting frames
Phase 4: look at still AND lyric — both required
Gate 1: eyeball stills/ before running Hailuo
Gate 2: approve each clip before assembly

beats.narration_text: Claude, Recut. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
